title: Messaging Outcomes, Delivery Claims, and Evidence · Type-System Trace: Ownership Handoff Map
summary: A team calls every background task reliable pub/sub, promises exactly-once delivery, and reports publish calls instead of stakeholder, ownership, loss, duplicate, latency, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ownership handoff map increment in messaging outcome, delivery claim, and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map message ownership from producer intent through local buffer, broker confirmation, queue, delivery, consumer acknowledgement, and durable effect.
State at-most-once, at-least-once, effectively-once effect, ordering, and durability claims without promising impossible end-to-end exactly-once delivery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Ownership Handoff Map · Theory Ownership Handoff Map
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 6561 in a regional incident workflow during one broker-node restart. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate local publish, broker ownership, and observable stakeholder effect; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Map message ownership from producer intent through local buffer, broker confirmation, queue, delivery, consumer acknowledgement, and durable effect.
